jokes.text: We got news, we've got terrible news.
jokes.setup: Someone bursting in to announce news
jokes.punchline: Redundantly calling it both 'news' and 'terrible news'
jokes.explanation: Minor absurdist redundancy fits the Gang's tendency to over-explain obvious things

dialogue.line: We got news, we've got terrible news.

jokes.text: That's what's good about a beer. Yeah. It just keeps cycling through...
jokes.setup: Characters discussing the merits of beer
jokes.punchline: The vague, meaningless observation about beer 'cycling through'
jokes.explanation: Establishes the characters' tendency toward pseudo-intellectual nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's what's good about a beer.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah. It just keeps cycling through...

jokes.text: Protein, omega numbers... Omega threes, fours and fives.
jokes.setup: Character listing health benefits of fish
jokes.punchline: Making up omega numbers that don't exist
jokes.explanation: Shows character's pseudo-intellectual pretensions while being completely wrong about basic nutrition

dialogue.line: Protein, omega numbers... Omega threes, fours and fives.

jokes.text: You get to eat all your scales.
jokes.setup: Continuing to list fish benefits
jokes.punchline: Suggesting you eat fish scales, which you definitely don't
jokes.explanation: Character's complete ignorance about basic food preparation

dialogue.line: You get to eat all your scales.

jokes.text: Yeah, not eating all reptiles, just the good ones.
jokes.setup: Being told not to eat reptiles
jokes.punchline: Agreeing but specifying they'll still eat the 'good' reptiles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gang logic - missing the point entirely while being oddly specific

dialogue.line: Yeah, not eating all reptiles, just the good ones.

jokes.text: You're gonna help us gather intel. Really? Let's do it, come on.
jokes.setup: Mac asking Dee to help with spy mission
jokes.punchline: Dee's unexpected enthusiasm for being included
jokes.explanation: Dee's desperate need for inclusion coming through

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You're gonna help us gather intel.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Really? Let's do it, come on.

jokes.text: You're completely unreliable, Dee, really. You're, like, all gangly and uncoordinated.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Dee's reliability
jokes.punchline: Pivoting to purely physical insults about her being gangly
jokes.explanation: Classic Gang meanness to Dee, though fairly standard for the show

dialogue.line: You're completely unreliable, Dee, really. You're, like, all gangly and uncoordinated.

jokes.text: Something stinks here, Charlie, and it ain't just the fish.
jokes.setup: Frank being suspicious about the Gang's behavior
jokes.punchline: Using the literal fish smell as a metaphor for something fishy
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed pun that works on multiple levels with the actual fish factory setting

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Something stinks here, Charlie, and it ain't just the fish.

jokes.text: Charlie, I trust the Chinese as far as I could throw their factory.
jokes.setup: Frank expressing distrust using a common idiom
jokes.punchline: Replacing 'them' with 'their factory' - an impossibly heavy building
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of familiar idiom that creates absurd mental image while maintaining the meaning

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Charlie, I trust the Chinese as far as I could throw their factory.

jokes.text: Well, you know the Chinese. It's like, business first, breathing second, goddamn it!
jokes.setup: Commenting on poor air quality in the factory
jokes.punchline: Fake profound observation about Chinese work ethic that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Mac's tendency to make sweeping cultural generalizations with false authority

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, you know the Chinese. It's like, business first, breathing second, goddamn it!

jokes.text: He'll love how 'pretty' and 'blonde' you are.
jokes.setup: Mac suggesting Dee flirt with the manager
jokes.punchline: Putting 'pretty' and 'blonde' in air quotes, implying she's neither
jokes.explanation: Typical Gang cruelty to Dee disguised as helpful advice

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He'll love how 'pretty' and 'blonde' you are.

jokes.text: I wanted to apologize about, um, snapping earlier today with words at you guys, and then I came here to make an apology... apology words.
jokes.setup: Charlie attempting to apologize normally
jokes.punchline: His bizarre phrasing 'snapping with words' and redundant 'apology words'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie-ism showing his childlike understanding of language and social interaction

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I wanted to apologize about, um, snapping earlier today with words at you guys, and then I came here to make an apology... apology words.

jokes.text: S... spider crawled up in my ear. It was living there. Right. Sends its thoughts to me.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking what's wrong with Charlie's ear
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely insane explanation involving telepathic spiders
jokes.explanation: Peak Charlie insanity delivered matter-of-factly, creating perfect absurdist comedy

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: S... spider crawled up in my ear. It was living there. Right. Sends its thoughts to me.

jokes.text: Who's Roger? Just... thinking about legally changing my name to Roger.
jokes.setup: Charlie saying 'Roger that' and being asked who Roger is
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical explanation about changing his name
jokes.explanation: Charlie's brain making bizarre connections between unrelated concepts

dialogue.line: Who's Roger?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Just... thinking about legally changing my name to Roger.

jokes.text: Spider, can I leave? To the left.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking permission from the spider in his ear
jokes.punchline: The spider apparently responding with positioning directions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that escalates the spider concept while serving the spy plot

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Spider, can I leave?

dialogue.speaker: Spider
dialogue.line: To the left.

jokes.text: No. I didn't get that. You chop fish, pull gut!
jokes.setup: Dee making a joke about protective masks
jokes.punchline: Worker's blunt, practical response showing complete disconnect
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Dee's attempts at humor and the worker's complete disinterest

dialogue.speaker: Fish Factory Worker
dialogue.line: No. I didn't get that. You chop fish, pull gut!

jokes.text: It got me! It got me good! Aah! It got in my mouth!
jokes.setup: Dee trying to seduce someone with her appearance
jokes.punchline: Fish guts apparently hitting her in the face/mouth
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that deflates Dee's attempt at being attractive

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: It got me! It got me good! Aah! It got in my mouth!

jokes.text: I'm to remember every man I've seen fall into a plate of spaghetti?
jokes.setup: Waiter reminding Mac of previous incident
jokes.punchline: Mac acting like making men fall into spaghetti is so common he can't remember specific instances
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation revealing Mac's complete lack of empathy while creating absurd mental image of routine spaghetti incidents

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm to remember every man I've seen fall into a plate of spaghetti?

jokes.text: Uh, hell no, lady, no. Uh, look, I realize this might all be moon talk to you, but I'm not touching fish guts with my hands, okay?
jokes.setup: Dee being told to clean up fish guts mess
jokes.punchline: Her entitled refusal and condescending explanation
jokes.explanation: Dee's complete unsuitability for any actual work

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Uh, hell no, lady, no. Uh, look, I realize this might all be moon talk to you, but I'm not touching fish guts with my hands, okay?

jokes.text: Just send it all over the place! Nobody will know.
jokes.setup: Dee dealing with fish guts mess
jokes.punchline: Her solution is to spread the mess everywhere instead of cleaning it
jokes.explanation: Dee's terrible problem-solving skills on full display

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Just send it all over the place! Nobody will know.

jokes.text: You're talking to me like you know me. Have we ever met?
jokes.setup: Same waiter approaching Frank after the spaghetti incident
jokes.punchline: Frank pretending not to remember despite being part of the same group
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag with Frank's shameless denial

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You're talking to me like you know me. Have we ever met?

jokes.text: Asians... and cream pies.
jokes.setup: Charlie revealing what he found in Mac and Dennis's search history
jokes.punchline: The innocent interpretation vs. the obvious sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the major misunderstanding that drives the episode's climax

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Asians... and cream pies.

jokes.text: Why do you look familiar to me? I'm the waiter you didn't tip earlier. Yeah, well, you're not gonna get tipped again with that attitude.
jokes.setup: Dennis not recognizing the waiter
jokes.punchline: Threatening to not tip again after being reminded he already stiffed him
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic inability to recognize consequences

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Why do you look familiar to me?

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: I'm the waiter you didn't tip earlier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you're not gonna get tipped again with that attitude.

jokes.text: I like cream pies as much as the next guy. Oh, shh... don't-don't say it out loud.
jokes.setup: Charlie wanting to join the 'cream pie business'
jokes.punchline: Mac thinking Charlie is being sexually explicit in public
jokes.explanation: Building the misunderstanding with perfect awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I like cream pies as much as the next guy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, shh... don't-don't say it out loud.

jokes.text: I do not want to taste your cream pie.
jokes.setup: Charlie offering to make Dennis a cream pie
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horrified reaction to what he thinks is a sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Dennis's revulsion perfect for the misunderstanding

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I do not want to taste your cream pie.

jokes.text: I'd love watching a kid do it. Dude, you are gonna get in a lot of trouble.
jokes.setup: Charlie talking about kids eating cream pies
jokes.punchline: Mac's horror at what sounds like something much darker
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment of the misunderstanding escalating to dark territory

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'd love watching a kid do it.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dude, you are gonna get in a lot of trouble.

jokes.text: What kind of cream pie are you talking about? What... kind of cream pie are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Growing confusion about what they're discussing
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous realization they're talking about different things
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of mutual realization about the misunderstanding

dialogue.line: What kind of cream pie are you talking about? What... kind of cream pie are you talking about?

jokes.text: Cream pies are a rich American tradition and also delicious.
jokes.setup: Montage beginning with patriotic narration
jokes.punchline: Overly serious treatment of something as mundane as cream pies
jokes.explanation: Absurd patriotic framing of desserts

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: Cream pies are a rich American tradition and also delicious.

jokes.text: Holy shit! Holy shit...
jokes.setup: Frank realizing the misunderstanding about cream pies
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous horrified realization from both sides
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the entire misunderstanding plot

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Holy shit! Holy shit...

jokes.text: We're out of snapper, Dee!
jokes.setup: Dee claiming her accidents helped them
jokes.punchline: Mac's exasperated response about the fish shortage she caused
jokes.explanation: Good callback tying together the fish shortage subplot

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're out of snapper, Dee!

jokes.text: I think what you're gonna find when you're spying on the general population is that everyone is just, uh... masturbating, constantly. I know I am.
jokes.setup: Frank commenting on their spying activities
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact conclusion about human behavior and personal admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - inappropriate oversharing delivered as profound wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I think what you're gonna find when you're spying on the general population is that everyone is just, uh... masturbating, constantly. I know I am.
